title: salvia coccinea var. pseudococcinea
intro: Salvia coccinea var. pseudococcinea, also known as red salvia, is a variety of the firecracker sage plant, a flowering perennial native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are said to aid in the treatment of wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Salvia coccinea var. pseudococcinea include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and support immune function. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as used in topical applications to harness its medicinal properties.
